requirements.text: WHEN a ticket subdocument is rendered, the system shall provide MarkdownContent a sourcePath derived from the selected SubDocument.filePath.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Acceptance Criteria: sourcePath is constructed from subdocument.filePath

requirements.text: WHEN markdown text contains a standalone relative .md reference with an optional heading anchor, the system shall convert only that reference into a document link while preserving the anchor.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Acceptance Criteria: .md#anchor links render as same-ticket subdocument links

requirements.text: WHEN an absolute ticket subdocument route includes an optional heading anchor, the system shall classify it as a document link for the current project.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Acceptance Criteria: resolved subdocument routes classify as document links

requirements.text: The document-reference conversion pattern shall not match markdown references embedded in URLs, email-like tokens, or protected markdown link/code placeholders.
The implementation shall preserve existing ticket, external, document, and anchor link classification behavior outside the MDT-155 cases.
The MDT-155 implementation shall not modify MDT-152 quick-search RED test files.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Acceptance Criteria: MDT-152 test files are not in the MDT-150 diff

requirements.text: WHEN a relative markdown link href targets a .md file with a heading anchor, the system shall classify it as a document link and preserve the anchor.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Acceptance Criteria: relative .md#section hrefs preserve the anchor during classification

requirements.text: The implementation shall preserve the existing frontend test suite with no regressions.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Acceptance Criteria: All 77+ existing tests still pass

scenarios.title: Subdocument render uses discovered filePath
scenarios.given: a ticket has a discovered subdocument with filePath MDT-155/requirements.trace.md
scenarios.when: the user opens the requirements.trace subdocument

scenarios.title: Standalone markdown references are converted tightly
scenarios.given: markdown contains standalone document references and non-reference tokens ending in .md
scenarios.then: only standalone relative markdown references become document links and anchors are preserved

scenarios.title: Absolute ticket subdocument routes classify as documents
scenarios.when: the link processor classifies the href
scenarios.then: the result is a current-project document link with documentPath requirements.trace.md and anchor #br-13

scenarios.title: Relative markdown href anchors remain document links
scenarios.given: a markdown link href is requirements.trace.md#br-13
scenarios.when: the link processor classifies the href
scenarios.then: the result is a document link with documentPath requirements.trace.md and anchor #br-13

obligations.title: TicketViewer resolves the active subdocument and passes its filePath as MarkdownContent sourcePath
markdownPreprocessor converts only standalone relative markdown references and preserves anchors
Existing linkProcessor document route classification remains covered by regression tests
MDT-152 quick-search test files are excluded from implementation ownership
Relative markdown hrefs with anchors classify as document links with anchors preserved
Existing frontend tests remain green after MDT-155 hardening

testPlans.title: TicketViewer passes selected SubDocument.filePath to MarkdownContent
markdownPreprocessor converts only standalone relative markdown references
linkProcessor classifies absolute ticket subdocument routes with anchors
Git diff excludes MDT-152 quick-search test files
linkProcessor classifies relative markdown hrefs with anchors

tasks.title: Use selected SubDocument.filePath for TicketViewer sourcePath
Verify link preservation, full regression, and MDT-152 exclusion
